---
title: "Practice: Process Diagrams"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# What We Practiced

The module showed how to:

- use a simple diagram when structure is the message
- sketch an analytic workflow or conceptual relationship
- keep diagrams small enough to explain verbally
- use Mermaid or `ggplot2` for simple diagrams

# Task

Create one simple process diagram showing one of these:

- how raw data become a final figure
- how a sample is filtered
- how variables might relate conceptually
- how a reader should move through a report

Helpful module reference: `modules/11_grab-bag/03_process-diagrams.qmd`.

# Work

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Next step]
  B --> C[Final step]
```

Optional: create a custom static diagram with `ggplot2`.

```{r}
# Optional ggplot diagram code here.
```

# Interpretation

Write briefly: what the diagram clarifies that a normal plot would not.
